response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

persona.time_onsocialmedia: You spend roughly a few times weekly on social media

response_parsed.opinion: As a fitness trainer in Lecce who spends a few times a week on social media, I've learned that fake content often comes from people trying to look smart or gain attention. My dad had a master's degree, so I know how to spot when someone's talking about things they don't understand. I used to scroll through Instagram and Facebook a lot, but now I only check once a week. When I see something questionable, I pause and ask myself: Is this true? Did someone else say it? Do I know the source? I also use my cooking skills to stay grounded—like when I make a simple meal, it reminds me to focus on real things. My high conscientiousness and low neuroticism help me stay calm and not get distracted by fake news. I've found that reading novels helps me think clearly too. If someone shares a video that looks too good to be true, I don't share it. Instead, I tell my friends to check the facts. Being an immigrant in Italy, I've seen how social media can bring people together, but it's important to be careful. I keep my social media time short so I can focus on my work and my family. Fake content can be tricky, but with a bit of common sense and a good dose of reality, we can manage it.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: The persona is a 36-year-old man from Lecce who is a fitness trainer, divorced, with one child, and an immigrant. He has high conscientiousness and extraversion, low agreeableness and neuroticism. His background includes a parent with a master's degree, which influences his critical thinking. He spends a few times a week on social media. The opinion should reflect his practical, no-nonsense approach, drawing from his hobbies (reading, cooking) and psychological traits. Focus on actionable steps like verifying information, limiting screen time, and using personal habits to stay grounded. Avoid technical jargon to keep it relatable. Ensure the tone matches his immigrant experience and fitness background. The response should be 250-500 words, continuous prose, no bullet points.
response_parsed.tone: Practical, grounded, slightly informal with a focus on real-world solutions
citations: Personal experience as a fitness trainer
